description:
## ELI5

The typer's SCC analysis was secretly coupled to "any-key spec
exists." When fz-duq introduced continuation fns mid-lowering (and
when a reverted attempt at fz-g8v did the same for multi-clause), the
new cont fns lacked any-key specs, so the typer's call graph silently
omitted their edges. Widening at fixed-point convergence never fired
across the new cont-fn boundaries; recursive callsites couldn't find
a covering spec at codegen time.

Decouple the call graph from spec registration — it's a *structural*
property of the IR, not a derived property of which specs happen to
be registered. The lower pass already does this correctly in
`annotate_back_edges` (`src/ir_lower.rs:615`); apply the same
discipline in the typer. Re-apply the reverted multi-clause body-cont
fix on top. Graduate `fixtures/actor_ring/`. Tighten fz-duq.2's
repro tests with stdout-based assertions while we're at it.

## Acceptance

- `fixtures/actor_ring/` runs correctly on interp + JIT + AOT.
- The minimal multi-clause CPS-split repro (`fn relay(0), do:
  receive() // fn relay(n), do: n // fn main(), do: print(relay(0))`)
  no longer panics at `fz_ir.rs:453`.
- All currently-passing tests stay green (594 + 147 + ...).
- Existing golden CLIFs for multi-clause fixtures (`multi_clause`,
  `mutual_recursion`, `fib_tailrec`, `tail_recursion`,
  `fib_tailrec`) remain byte-identical post-inline. If any drift
  appears it's evidence the inliner can't collapse the new cont-fn
  shape for recursive multi-clause — investigate separately as an
  inliner ticket, do not bless until understood.
- fz-duq.2's three repro tests assert stdout equality, not IR shape.

## DAG of atomic steps

```
[.1] typer: build_call_graph structural-only
         │
         ▼
[.2] lower_multi_clause re-applies continuation-fn body fix
         │
         ▼
[.3] fixtures: graduate actor_ring + multi-clause body-call repros
[.4] tighten fz-duq.2 repros (run_and_capture helper)  ◄── independent of .1/.2/.3
```

## What this fix removes

- The any-key-spec dependency in `ir_typer::build_call_graph`
  (`src/ir_typer.rs:50`) — its `specs` parameter, its any-key
  lookup at `:60-63`, and the `fn_constants`-based closure target
  resolution at `:89, :95`. After this, the typer's call graph is
  derived purely from terminator/Prim::MakeClosure structure, the
  same way `ir_lower::annotate_back_edges:615` already does it.
- The "KNOWN LIMITATION" comment block in `lower_multi_clause`
  added by fz-duq.4 to document the deferral.
- The per-clause inline-body-then-Return stanza in
  `lower_multi_clause`. Replaced with mint_cont_fn + finalize_arm,
  the same pattern as fz-duq.2/.3/.4.
- Three weak shape-only repro tests in `src/ir_lower.rs::tests`:
  `lower_if_constant_cond_with_call_in_arm_no_panic`,
  `lower_if_tail_call_in_arm_preserved`,
  `lower_if_unnarrowed_cond_with_tail_call_in_arm`. Replaced with
  stdout-equality tests using a new `run_and_capture` helper.
- The hidden coupling itself: "the SCC of a fn depends on whether
  the typer has registered an any-key spec for it." This is the
  load-bearing removal — it's why future cont-fn-introducing
  lowering won't silently regress.

## Children

- `.1` typer build_call_graph structural-only (no spec dependency)
- `.2` lower_multi_clause continuation-fn body
- `.3` fixtures graduate (actor_ring + multi-clause body-call)
- `.4` run_and_capture + tighten fz-duq.2 repros

## Walkthrough — issues surfaced

1. **`build_call_graph` is called at two sites** (`:472` with empty
   specs map; `:577` with the live specs). After .1 both sites are
   updated; the `specs` parameter is dropped. The line-:472 case
   already passes empty so the behavior is equivalent there — only
   line :577 actually loses information today (which is what we
   want to recover).

2. **Closure target resolution.** Today's `build_call_graph` resolves
   CallClosure targets via `fn_constants` when available. Dropping
   the any-key dependency means we lose this resolution at
   graph-build time. The lost edges only matter for SCC analysis
   on closure-mediated recursion (Y combinator style), which no
   current fz code uses. Add a structural over-approximation if
   needed: edges from each fn to every `Prim::MakeClosure(lam, _)`
   target visible in the same fn's body. (Already there at `:68-70`,
   so this part stays.)

3. **fz-duq.4.5's pattern_var_records_source_name_and_span failure.**
   The reverted multi-clause refactor also broke this test because
   prelude cont fns minted during multi-clause prelude lowering
   (e.g., `print`'s two clauses) got FnIds >= prelude_fn_id_cutoff
   and polluted user var_meta. Fix as part of .2: lower prelude fn
   bodies *before* registering user fn FnIds, then bump cutoff.
   Single line addition to `lower_program_full`.

4. **Tight goldens.** Existing multi-clause fixtures
   (`multi_clause`, `mutual_recursion`, `fib_tailrec`,
   `tail_recursion`) carry golden CLIF + specs. Post-.2 they
   *should* be byte-identical post-inline. If they drift, the
   inliner isn't collapsing the new cont-fn shape for recursive
   multi-clause. Don't bless; investigate. Likely root: inliner
   refuses to inline a cont fn that tail-calls its caller's caller
   (a self-loop pattern). Fix in inliner if so.

5. **AOT path for actor_ring.** actor_ring uses spawn + receive +
   self()-capture. AOT supports these per the BEAM-correct
   scheduler work (fz-sched). Graduate as `[jit, interp, aot]`.
title: follow-up to fz-duq: multi-clause + typer SCC fix + ancillary
